summary: Soundboard recording (with a few audience tracks) is good overall, while the Boulder‑72 show dazzles with a thunderstorm‑driven energy, stellar jams on The Other One, Cold Rain & Snow and China>Rider.
review: The performance captured on this tape is one of the most celebrated from the spring‑1972 Colorado run – rain‑soaked but electrified, featuring monster jams on The Other One, a soaring Cold Rain & Snow, and a blistering China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider.  Technically the source is mainly soundboard (the first eight cuts are audience only) and while early tracks are muddy or have drop‑outs from stage arcing, the bulk of the set sounds clear, stereo and well‑balanced.

ai_rating.rationale: Average reviewer rating is ~4.3★; most comments praise the music while noting only early‑set recording flaws, yielding a strong overall score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: First ~8 tracks are audience recordings (muffled, occasional drop‑outs). After the rain‑break the mix switches to soundboard stereo hi‑fi; occasional tape dropout and a muddy “underwater” section but overall clear and balanced.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road > Not Fade Away
show_quality.setlist_flow: Three‑set marathon interrupted by a rainstorm; the band returned with renewed vigor, delivering a tight first set, a jam‑heavy second set that recovers after an early Bertha glitch, and a soaring third set highlighted by The Other One and the NFA sandwich.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for scorching guitar work – wailing on China>Rider, fierce leads on The Other One and Not Fade Away, and overall “flawless” tone.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for solid bass foundation and a “realignment” moment during The Other One that helped drive the jam.
band_member_comments.Bob: Garcia (often called Bobby) is highlighted for stage banter, humor (Tina Turner impression) and strong rhythm playing; Bob Weir’s vocals on Johnny B. Goode are praised.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith & Donna (new members) receive positive nods for their keyboard contributions throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is mentioned only implicitly as part of the solid, “rock‑solid” rhythm section.
